brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +36.1% since Feb 27 — Brent $96.66 (Apr 10 close) — down 10%+ for the week from $118 at end of Q1 — but physical dated Brent at $131.97 ($35 premium over futures) reflecting extreme physical scarcity — Islamabad talks in progress
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: ~$96.66/bbl (Friday). Down 10%+ for the week from $118 at end of Q1; down from the $144 dated-Brent spot peak on Apr 7. CRITICAL: Physical dated Brent traded at $131.97/bbl — a $35 premium over futures reflecting extreme physical scarcity due to Hormuz restrictions. IRGC established 'Tehran Tollbooth' — alternative channel north of Larak Island requiring prior permission, charging up to $2M/vessel (yuan or crypto), capping at 15 ships/day. 500-700 vessels stranded (Lloyd's List). 13M bpd shut in (Energy Aspects). The gap between dated ($132) and futures ($97) is the market's measure of how fragile this ceasefire really is. Islamabad talks in progress — first direct US-Iran face-to-face since 1979. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / ADNOC / Lloyd's List / Energy Aspects.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +43.8% since Feb 27 — WTI $95.63 (Apr 10 close) — ceasefire holding ~96 hrs on Iran front but Hormuz 'Tehran Tollbooth' caps traffic at 15 ships/day — Islamabad talks underway
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: ~$95.63/bbl (Friday). Down sharply from $115 on Apr 7 pre-ceasefire. Ceasefire optimism drove best weekly decline in oil since November. However, 'Tehran Tollbooth' caps Hormuz traffic at 15 ships/day with $2M tolls — versus pre-war 135/day. Gas at $4.153/gal nationally (AAA). March CPI showed gasoline prices surging 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Diesel at $5.683. Five states exceed $5/gallon (California at $5.92). Islamabad talks in progress — first direct US-Iran engagement since 1979. Hormuz reopening central to negotiations. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.173 / +39.4% since Feb 27 — $4.153 nationally (AAA) — March CPI: gas +21.2% MoM (largest spike since 1967) — 5 states above $5 — California $5.92 — diesel $5.683
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.153/gal nationally (AAA). Five states exceed $5/gallon: California at $5.92. Diesel at $5.683. March CPI (released Apr 10) confirmed gasoline prices surged 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Energy overall +10.9% MoM, +12.5% YoY. Airlines hiked checked bag fees to $45 across the board. Delta warned it would 'meaningfully' cut growth plans due to fuel costs. BLS data quality caveat: fall 2025 government shutdown (43 days) disrupted data collection — carry-forward methodology may bias inflation readings. Gasoline lags crude by 1-2 weeks. Source: AAA / BLS / EIA / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -62 pts / -0.9% from pre-war — S&P 6,816.89 (Apr 10 close, -0.11%) — best week since Nov (+3.6%) — VIX 19.23 — Nasdaq +4.7% — markets round-tripped to near pre-war levels
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 10 close: 6,816.89 (-0.11% on day, +3.6% for week — best since November). Nasdaq gained 4.7% for week (8-day winning streak). Dow +3.0% (positive for 2026). Markets round-tripped to roughly pre-war levels — S&P less than 3% below late-January all-time high. VIX collapsed to 19.23 (from 26 pre-ceasefire). Gold: $4,787/oz (3rd consecutive weekly gain, +$1,568 YoY). 10Y Treasury: 4.31% (up from 3.95% two months ago on inflation fears). March CPI: headline 3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM, above 0.7% consensus); core 2.6% (below consensus). Gas +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967). UMich consumer sentiment 47.6 (all-time record low). LMT ~$614 (+30% YTD, PAC-3 $4.7B contract). RTX ~$202. XOM $152.51 (+42% YTD through Q1). CVX $188.55 (+37% YTD). DAL ~$64 (+12.5% Apr 8 earnings beat). UAL ~$91.35. BoA revised 2026 global growth down 40bps to 3.1%. Fed holds 3.50-3.75%; markets price no rate cuts for 2026. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / UMich / BoA.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — CEASEFIRE ~96 HOURS — full force posture maintained — Islamabad talks in progress — Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — no official ceasefire burn rate released
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 43: ~$800M/day — ceasefire reduces active combat costs but CENTCOM maintains full force posture. No official 'ceasefire burn rate' released. Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes estimates $2B/day including all operational costs, calling it 'the tip of the iceberg.' CSIS: ~$30B total. AEI: $22.3-31B through five weeks. ~50,000+ US service members participated. Bush CSG approaching Gibraltar — ETA Red Sea Apr 14-18. Total battle force: 291 ships (108 deployed, 83 underway). Pentagon supplemental shrunk from $200B to expected $80-100B. LMT awarded $4.7B PAC-3 contract Apr 10. 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29. Congress returns Apr 14. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WaPo / USNI Fleet Tracker.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (43 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$50.1B through Day 43 — CSIS: ~$30B — AEI: $22.3-31B — Harvard: $2B/day, $1 trillion long-term — WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer) — Pentagon supplemental shrunk from $200B to $80-100B
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12, ~$30B total. AEI: $22.3-31B for five weeks. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day current rate, $1 trillion long-term. WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer). Pentagon supplemental shrunk from $200B to $80-100B (WaPo Apr 7). FY2027 budget includes 150% increase ($47B boost) in munitions procurement. War approval eroded to ~34% (Economist/YouGov, CNN/SSRS). Only 15% support ground troops. Trump Iran handling: 33% (CNN). UMich consumer sentiment record low 47.6. No Kings 3 protest Mar 28: 8-9 million across 3,300+ events. 70+ House Democrats called for Trump's removal. 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WarCosts.org / WaPo / CNN / YouGov / UMich.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ~96 HRS — 35,500+ targets cumulative — CENTCOM: 13,000+ targets, 155+ warships sunk (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses destroyed — 50,000+ US service members — no strikes on Iran since Apr 7
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. Iran air force at zero flights/day. ~50,000+ US service members participated. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 40. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~96 hours). Israel continues Lebanon operations: 120 Hezbollah sites struck in 24 hours on Apr 10. Islamabad talks in progress — first direct US-Iran face-to-face since 1979. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 357-381 WIA (Pentagon figures internally inconsistent) — Intercept: undercounting excludes 200+ Ford fire sailors — ceasefire ~96 hrs — no new casualties since Apr 7
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death, with 357-381 wounded (Pentagon figures internally inconsistent across DCAS pages). The Intercept exposed discrepancies: exclusion of 200+ sailors treated after Ford fire and at least one confirmed combat-support death omitted from official rolls. Most injuries are TBI from blast overpressure. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. 6 at Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1), 6 in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Mar 12), 1 in Saudi Arabia. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion — rescue involved 155-176 aircraft, CIA deception, MC-130J landing on Iranian airstrip. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 357-381 WIA (Pentagon figures internally inconsistent across DCAS pages) — Intercept: undercounting excludes 200+ Ford fire sailors and combat-support death — most injuries TBI — ceasefire ~96 hrs — no new WIA since Apr 7
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. Pentagon reports 357-381 wounded (figures internally inconsistent across different DCAS pages). The Intercept exposed discrepancies: exclusion of 200+ sailors treated after Ford fire (Mar 12) and at least one confirmed combat-support death omitted from official rolls. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~96 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,000+ / HRANA: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified) / Iran Intl: 4,700+ security forces / Hengaw: 6,620 military — IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC — no deaths since ceasefire Apr 7 — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources. Iran Health Ministry: 2,000+ killed (widely considered dramatic undercount). HRANA (US-based): 3,636 total deaths — 1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified. Hengaw (Norway-based): 6,620 Iranian military dead alone. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces killed with 20,880 injured. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. Displacement: 3.2M internally displaced in Iran; 81,700 crossed into Turkey (67,000 returned); 206,500 Syrians and 36,800 Lebanese fled from Lebanon into Syria. UNHCR funding at just 15% of requirements for Iran-Afghanistan corridor. 307 health facilities damaged. Pasteur Institute inoperable. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / IDF / WHO / UNHCR.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,888-1,953 killed, 6,303+ wounded, 1.05M displaced (35% children) — Israel struck 120 Hezbollah sites in 24 hrs Apr 10 — 303 killed on Apr 8 (Operation Eternal Darkness, 33 children) — 14 killed Apr 10 incl. 13 State Security in Nabatieh — Hezbollah fires 100+ rockets since ceasefire
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative: 1,888-1,953 killed, 6,303+ wounded, 1.05 million displaced (35% children). DEADLIEST DAY: April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 303 killed, 1,150 wounded including 33 children. 100+ airstrikes in ~10 minutes using 50 fighter jets targeting central Beirut (no prior warning), Bekaa Valley cemetery during funeral, Nabatieh. Lebanon declared April 9 national day of mourning. April 10: 14 killed including 13 Lebanese State Security personnel in Nabatieh government building. IDF struck 120 Hezbollah sites in 24 hours. Trump asked Netanyahu to scale back. Israel authorized direct talks with Lebanon at State Department next week. Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets since ceasefire — 30+ on April 10 alone hitting Kibbutz Misgav Am, school in Deir al-Asad, Safed, Kiryat Shmona. 1 IDF NCO severely wounded by suicide drone. WHO warned hospitals could run out of trauma kits within days. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNICEF / WHO / HRW.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 7.5M+ — Iran 3.2M IDPs — Lebanon 1.05M (35% children) — 81,700 Iranians crossed into Turkey (67,000 returned) — 206,500 Syrians fled Lebanon into Syria — UNHCR funding: 15% (Iran-Afghanistan) / 14% (Lebanon)
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 3.2 million internally displaced. 81,700 Iranians crossed into Turkey (67,000 returned). Lebanon: 1.05 million displaced (35% children). 206,500 Syrians and 36,800 Lebanese fled from Lebanon into Syria. UNHCR funding at just 15% of requirements for Iran-Afghanistan corridor and 14% for Lebanon. Ceasefire holding on Iran may slow displacement but Lebanon excluded — continued Israeli operations. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — Hormuz 'Tehran Tollbooth' — only 10-16 ships in first 3 days post-ceasefire vs 135/day pre-war — 500-700 vessels stranded — 325 tankers — 20,000+ seafarers — tolls up to $2M/vessel in yuan or crypto
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz: IRGC established 'Tehran Tollbooth' — alternative channel north of Larak Island requiring prior permission, charging up to $2M/vessel (payable in yuan or cryptocurrency), capping traffic at 15 ships/day. Only ~10-16 ships transited in first 3 days post-ceasefire vs ~135/day pre-war. 500-700 vessels over 10,000 DWT stranded (Lloyd's List), including 325 tankers and ~230 oil-laden ships. 20,000+ seafarers aboard stranded vessels. War risk insurance at ~8x pre-war levels. All 12 P&I clubs withdrew war risk coverage — 'de facto closure by insurance.' Iran navy released map suggesting standard lanes may have been mined. Physical dated Brent at $132 vs futures $97 = $35 premium = physical scarcity signal. Source: ADNOC / Lloyd's List / IMO / IATA / Energy Aspects.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 244+ (15% of casualties under 18) — UNICEF Lebanon: 600 children killed or injured — 33 children killed Apr 8 Operation Eternal Darkness — Minab school: ~175 killed — HRW: Minab = war crime — ceasefire on Iran but Lebanon excluded
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran (15% of casualties under 18). UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured in Lebanon. April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness': 303 killed including 33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Ceasefire holding on Iranian territory ~96 hours. Israel continues Lebanon operations — 120 Hezbollah sites struck Apr 10. WHO warned hospitals could run out of trauma kits within days. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Amnesty International.
